code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Group triples by subject to allow multiple entities
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_map[t.subject].append(t)

    # Predicate → phrase templates that embed the subject for fluent sentences
    templates = {
        # Airport‑specific predicates
        "cityServed": "{subj} serves {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "{subj} is {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "{subj} is {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "{subj} is {obj} feet above sea level",
        "runwayLength": "the runway length of {subj} is {obj} metres",
        "runwayName": "the runway name of {subj} is {obj}",
        "location": "{subj} is located in {obj}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "{subj} is operated by {obj}",
        "country": "{subj} is in {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "{subj} is part of {obj}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "the ICAO code of {subj} is {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": "the location identifier of {subj} is {obj}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "the IATA code of {subj} is {obj}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "the first runway of {subj} is {obj} feet long",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "the first runway of {subj} is {obj} metres long",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "the first runway surface of {subj} is {obj}",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "the first runway number of {subj} is {obj}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "the runway surface of {subj} is {obj}",
        # Additional predicates
        "jurisdiction": "{subj} has jurisdiction in {obj}",
        # Generic entity predicates (subject embedded)
        "leader": "{subj}'s leader is {obj}",
        "capital": "{subj}'s capital is {obj}",
        "language": "{subj}'s language is {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "{subj}'s official language is {obj}",
        "currency": "{subj}'s currency is {obj}",
        "owner": "{subj}'s owner is {obj}",
        "foundingYear": "{subj} was founded in {obj}",
        "founder": "{subj} was founded by {obj}",
        "postalCode": "{subj}'s postal code is {obj}",
        "areaCode": "{subj}'s area code is {obj}",
        "largestCity": "{subj}'s largest city is {obj}",
        "countySeat": "{subj}'s county seat is {obj}",
        "mayor": "{subj}'s mayor is {obj}",
        "leaderParty": "{subj}'s leader party is {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "{subj}'s leader title is {obj}",
        "chief": "{subj}'s chief is {obj}",
        "hubAirport": "{subj}'s hub airport is {obj}",
        "regionServed": "{subj} serves the region {obj}",
        "nativeName": "{subj}'s native name is {obj}",
        # Biological classification predicates
        "class": "{subj}'s class is {obj}",
        "division": "{subj}'s division is {obj}",
        "order": "{subj}'s order is {obj}",
        "species": "{subj}'s species is {obj}",
        "demonym": "{subj}'s demonym is {obj}",
    }

    sentences = []
    for subject, tlist in subject_map.items():
        parts = []
        for t in tlist:
            tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                # Strip surrounding quotes from objects if present
                obj = t.object.strip('"')
                parts.append(tmpl.format(obj=obj))
            else:
                # Fallback generic phrase
                pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
                obj = t.object.strip('"')
                parts.append(f"{pred} {obj}")
        # Combine parts into a fluent sentence
        if parts:
            if len(parts) == 1:
                sentence = f"{subject} {parts[0]}."
            else:
                sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1] + "."
            sentences.append(sentence)

    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
